input.background: A survey selects an SRS of n=500 people from a town of 55,000. The sample shows an average of 2.30 health problems per person (standard deviation =1.65). Based on this information, say whether each of the following statements is true or false. Explain your reasoning in each instance.
input.question: (a) The standard deviation of the sample mean is 0.074.

output.answer: True. The standard deviation of \(\bar{x}\) = the standard error of the mean \(= \frac{1.65}{\sqrt{500}}=0.0740\).

sample_size.description: The number of people selected in the simple random sample (SRS).

population_size.description: The total number of people in the town from which the sample was drawn.

sample_mean_health_problems.description: The average number of health problems per person in the sample.

sample_standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the number of health problems per person in the sample.

standard_error.description: The standard deviation of the sample mean, also known as the standard error.
